Styrax or Styrax tonkinensis is a plant species in the Styracaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The resin of this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Styrax include benzyl benzoate, benzaldehyde, and vanillin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Styrax include resin extracts, oils, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues.
